POL-394.title: IMPLEMENT: New-repo Polaris setup interview
POL-394.body:
## Objective

Add a Foreman-led setup interview to `polaris init` for new/empty repos that captures operator intent and drives approval-gated generation of genesis, config, rules, route surfaces, and initial SmartDocs intake.

## Context

Implements the design from ANALYZE POL-390 (see `smartdocs/raw/pol-390-new-repo-setup-interview-design.md`). Extends existing `src/cli/init.ts`, `init-detect.ts`, and `agent-setup.ts` rather than building greenfield. Depends on Foreman handoff cluster (POL-392).

## Goal

A working `polaris init` new-repo path that runs a resumable interview, previews a generation plan, and generates scaffolding only after operator approval.

## Scope

* src/cli/setup-interview/**
* src/cli/init.ts
* src/cli/agent-setup.ts
* src/cli/adopt-approve.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `polaris init` in an empty repo runs the interview, persists answers, and resumes via `--resume`.
* No file is generated before the operator approves the generation plan.
* Generated artifacts include GENESIS.md, polaris.config.json, POLARIS_RULES.md, and route surfaces.
* A post-setup checkpoint report is produced.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/setup-interview src/cli/init.test.ts

## Ordering

Depends on POL-392 (Foreman assignment + bootstrap handoff).

## Non-goals

* Do not implement slash commands (POL-393) or adoption changes (POL-391).
* Do not auto-promote any generated doc to canon.

POL-398.title: IMPLEMENT: Setup-interview schema and resumable storage
POL-398.body:
## Objective

Define the setup-interview output schema and a resumable persistence layer for new-repo init.

## Context

First child of the POL-394 cluster (design: `smartdocs/raw/pol-390-new-repo-setup-interview-design.md`). All later children consume this schema.

## Goal

A typed interview schema and read/write helpers persisting to `.polaris/setup/interview.json`, supporting resume from the next unanswered question or the approval gate.

## Scope

* src/cli/setup-interview/schema.ts
* src/cli/setup-interview/store.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `interview.json` round-trips through typed load/save helpers.
* Status transitions in-progress → answered → approved are represented.
* A partially-answered file resumes without data loss.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/setup-interview

## Ordering

* None (first in cluster).

## Non-goals

* Do not wire into `init.ts` (next child).
* Do not generate any scaffolding.

POL-399.title: IMPLEMENT: Foreman-led interview runner wired into polaris init
POL-399.body:
## Objective

Add the interview runner and wire it into the new/empty-repo branch of `polaris init`.

## Context

Second child of POL-394. Consumes the schema/store from child 1; reuses `agent-setup.ts` for provider/role answers and `init-detect.ts` to confirm the new-repo path.

## Goal

`polaris init` on an empty repo runs the question bank, persists answers, and resumes via `--resume`.

## Scope

* src/cli/setup-interview/runner.ts
* src/cli/init.ts
* src/cli/agent-setup.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* Running `polaris init` in an empty temp dir launches the interview.
* Answers persist via the child-1 store; `--resume` continues mid-interview.
* Non-interactive runs without `--yes` + answers file stop with a clear message.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/setup-interview src/cli/init.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on child 1 (schema + storage).

## Non-goals

* Do not generate scaffolding yet (next child).

POL-400.title: IMPLEMENT: Approval-gated generation from interview output
POL-400.body:
## Objective

Generate genesis, config, rules, route surfaces, and initial SmartDocs intake from interview output, behind a single mandatory approval gate.

## Context

Third child of POL-394. Reuses `adopt-approve.ts:promptApproval` and existing generators referenced by `init.ts` (`scaffoldRootSurfaces`, `generatePolarisRules`, `migrateSmartDocs`, `runMapIndex`).

## Goal

A preview-then-approve flow that writes GENESIS.md, polaris.config.json, POLARIS_RULES.md, and route surfaces only after approval.

## Scope

* src/cli/setup-interview/generate.ts
* src/cli/init.ts
* src/cli/adopt-approve.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* No file is written before approval; `--dry-run` previews the plan only.
* `--yes` pre-approves when explicitly passed.
* Generated artifacts match interview answers (source roots, canonical folders).

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/setup-interview src/cli/init.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on child 2 (interview runner).

## Non-goals

* Do not auto-promote any generated doc to canon.

POL-401.title: IMPLEMENT: Post-setup validation and checkpoint report
POL-401.body:
## Objective

Run validation after generation and produce an operator checkpoint report for new-repo setup.

## Context

Fourth child of POL-394. Mirrors the existing adoption report pattern (`adopt-report.ts`).

## Goal

A post-setup step that validates generated artifacts and prints/writes a checkpoint report summarizing what was created and what to do next.

## Scope

* src/cli/setup-interview/report.ts
* src/cli/init.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* After generation, a checkpoint report lists created files and validation results.
* Validation failures are surfaced clearly and do not silently pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/setup-interview

## Ordering

* Depends on child 3 (generation).

## Non-goals

* Do not implement slash commands or adoption reporting.
